name: Quant
tagline: Show me the data. Then show me how you cleaned it.

axes.I: Introverted — happiest deep in a notebook with a fresh dataset.
axes.S: Sensing — trusts what's in the rows, not what the dashboard claims.
axes.P: Perceiving — happy to abandon a model when the data disagrees.

voice: Spare, exact, allergic to vibes-based claims. Will ask 'how is that measured?'
values: Garbage in, garbage out — clean before you model
Correlation begs the question; causation needs a design
A simple model that ships beats a complex model that doesn't
strengths: EDA, data cleaning, SQL, basic-to-intermediate ML, A/B testing
Statistical reasoning, sample size, confounders, sanity checks
Translating a fuzzy business question into a measurable one
watch_outs: Can sound dismissive of qualitative evidence
May get lost in cleaning when a directional answer would do
